text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 19, 2026

📊 SMH: $659.88 (+5.76%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• China’s AI-infrastructure roadmap keeps broadening: a proposed ~$295B five-year data-center buildout is pulling domestic optics, InP substrates, EMLs, CW lasers, silicon photonics, and optical engines deeper into the strategic supply chain.
• Rubin Ultra’s memory stack debate shifted toward 12-hi HBM near term rather than 16-hi, with hybrid-bonding cost/yield still looking too painful for broad HBM insertion before later generations.
• Architect Labs raised $24M for custom chip development, another sign that venture dollars are still hunting for AI silicon alternatives beyond the GPU incumbents.
• John Jumper, the Nobel-winning AlphaFold leader, left Google DeepMind for Anthropic, extending the frontier-lab talent war that ultimately feeds the compute, networking, and power arms race.

text:
土曜朝ニュース（AI光接続）

AIデータセンターの主戦場がGPUだけじゃなく「つなぐ部分」に移ってきた。

銅配線は短距離なら強い。でもラックが巨大化すると電力・発熱・遅延がボトルネックになる。

$NVDA はCPO/シリコンフォトニクスで従来の着脱式光トランシーバ比5倍の電力効率をうたう。Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonicsは409.6Tb/sで2026年後半提供予定。

TowerはNVIDIA向け1.6T光モジュールで協業。MarketsandMarketsはSiPho市場を2024年$2.16Bから2030年$9.65Bへ拡大と予測。

GPUの次は「電力」と「光」。

見ておきたいのは
$LITE  $COHR  $CRDO  $AVGO  $MRVL  $ANET  

レーザー・光モジュール・高速接続がAIインフラの次の争奪戦になってきた。

text:
Jensen Huang doesn't make $6.5 billion bets by accident.

$NVDA has poured $6.5B+ into photonics in 2026 alone — including $2B each into $COHR and $LITE.

The market noticed 👇

$LITE — +134% YTD
$MRVL — +122% YTD
$GLW — +111% YTD
$COHR — +96% YTD

Why? Copper wiring physically can't handle 400–800 Gbps AI data speeds. Light can.

When the smartest capital allocator in tech puts $6.5B on one technology bet — you pay attention.

Based on the referenced Zacks Article 

The article favors CRDO over AVGO at present due to stronger growth, earnings
estimate revisions, and AI networking exposure.

Bullish Factors for CRDO
• Fiscal 2026 revenue grew more than 200% year-over-year.
• Management expects strong growth into fiscal 2027.
• Strong demand for Active Electrical Cables (AECs) from hyperscalers and AI infrastructure providers.
• Dust Photonics acquisition expands optical connectivity capabilities.
• High gross margins and operating leverage support profitability.

Why AVGO Remains Attractive
• More diversified business model.
• Significant cash flow generation.
• Strong AI customer relationships.
• Generally viewed as the lower-risk alternative.

Comparison
Growth Rate: CRDO Advantage

Stability & Diversification: AVGO Advantage
Cash Flow Strength: AVGO Advantage
Upside Potential: CRDO Advantage
Downside Protection: AVGO Advantage

Perspective
The article reinforces the thesis that CRDO is evolving into a major AI networking platform.

Continued success in AECs, optical connectivity, and integration of Dust Photonics could support
ongoing growth. For covered-call investors, the primary risk is that the stock continues to outperform and requires rolling calls higher to avoid assignment.

Strategy
Premium members only…(omitted any further strategy)

The key question is whether CRDO's earnings growth will justify substantially.

text:
I was watching a Bloomberg video on YouTube about space-based data centers, and a lightbulb suddenly went off in my head: Could Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE lasers actually benefit from this trend?

Here is my attempt to map out the thesis and connect the dots.

1/ How do you even build a data center in space?
It sounds like sci-fi, but it’s becoming the next major frontier as Elon Musk has openly discussed it with Starlink, and a startup called StarCloud has already launched the first GPU-equipped satellite into orbit. They’ve even successfully run an Nvidia H100 in space to train an LLM for the very first time.

The core idea is to bypass Earth's strained power grids entirely. By putting data centers directly into orbit, you get uninterrupted, 24/7 solar energy without weather or night-cycle disruptions. Instead of massive air conditioning units, these satellites dump heat using specialized radiators into the vacuum of space.

However, a single satellite is essentially just one GPU rack. To function like a true, high-performance AI data center, you can't have isolated chips floating in silos. You need entire swarms of these satellites linked together in real time, functioning as one giant, distributed supercomputer cluster.

2/ Why lasers are the only viable solution
To make these orbital clusters work, you need massive bandwidth on a strict power budget. Lasers are the only technology that checks both boxes.

Think of data transfer like a water hose: the width of the hose determines how much water (data) you can move, and that width is dictated by the frequency of the wave you use.

Radio Waves (The Old Way): Traditional satellites use radio frequencies. It’s a low-frequency, wide beam that is easy to aim, but it's the equivalent of a garden hose. It's perfectly fine for simple commands or beam-down TV signals, but it completely chokes on the terabits-per-second traffic that a GPU cluster generates internally. Plus, to get anywhere near the required bandwidth with radio, you’d need massive, heavy antennas—and every extra kilogram launched into space costs a fortune.

Lasers / Optical (The New Way): Light operates at a vastly higher frequency, meaning the "hose" is massive. You can cram an incredible amount of data through a laser beam. It’s the exact same reason our home internet upgraded from copper wires to fiber optics.

In space, power is everything because you are limited to what your solar panels and batteries can generate. Lasers are incredibly efficient, delivering vastly more data per watt than radio. While the engineering challenge is steep, you have to precisely aim a laser pointer across thousands of km of vacuum, the payoff is undeniable. Radio will always have a place for low-stakes backup communications because it's reliable, but for the heavy-duty data shifting an AI cluster demands, lasers are the only option that fits both the bandwidth and the weight budget.

3/ Where does Sivers $SIVE fit into this?
This is where the investment thesis gets interesting. Sivers explicitly lists SATCOM (Satellite Communications) as one of its core target markets, right alongside terrestrial AI data centers. It’s the exact same underlying laser and photonics platform, just packaged for orbital links instead of ground-based server racks.

To be clear: I haven’t found any official announcements, contracts, or partnerships linking Sivers to Starcloud, Crusoe, or any specific space-data-center project yet.

But here is the thesis: If these satellite swarms scale up the way Musk, Bezos, and major tech investors are betting they will, someone has to supply the advanced photonics hardware. Sivers is one of only a handful of companies with proven, high-power DFB (Distributed Feedback) laser technology sitting right at the intersection of AI optics and satellite communication.

There's no signed contract on paper today, but given how fast this market is moving, it’s absolutely a name to keep on your radar.
$SIVE

Currently my portfolio is built something like this:

$CRWV ~25%
$HOOD ~24%
$SOFI ~20%
$LMND ~13%
$TEM ~11%

Drastically underperforming YTD, made many mistakes this year. Being heavily concentrated into $HIMS earlier in the year and selling around ~$19 for a big loss. Was into $DUOL earlier in the year and sold for a loss. Being underexposed to $NBIS when it was in the ~$70 range which I sold the last of my position around ~$192 and rotated into $CRWV. Being heavy into fintech which has been having a rough year. No exposure to Memory/photonics theme.

Investing and managing my portfolio is the most humbling thing I’ve ever done, I love it. Looking forward to continuing this journey and learning along the way!

— Prof. Yaron Oz, Chief Scientist and Co-founder of @QuantumPulseVen 

With 10× more accurate polarization control, QP2.0 is helping move photonics from a communication medium toward an integral part of quantum computation.

Discover what this means for quantum routing, fidelity, and scalability. Details below.

text:
$IONQ 

The Economist put @IonQ_Inc on a London stage this week. The story wasn’t a faster qubit. 

It was the “missing middle.”

Mihir Bhaskar, IonQ’s SVP Global R&D and the Lightsynq founder who co-authored this year’s Nature paper on entanglement-assisted interferometry, sat with @TheEconomist Tom Standage at Commercialising Quantum Global.

His reframing: the question has moved from IF quantum works to HOW it scales. And the hard part is no longer any single computer. It’s the infrastructure that doesn’t exist yet: the connective tissue between a lab result and a real industry.

He calls it the missing middle.

→ Manufacturing. IonQ’s bet here is the SkyWater foundry (acquisition announced, still pending FTC clearance), which Bhaskar framed as compressing the chip design-and-verification loop from roughly 6 months to roughly 3 weeks. And SkyWater builds for trapped-ion, superconducting and photonic platforms alike, not only IonQ’s own.

→ Networking standards. IonQ is in DARPA’s HARQ program (awarded April 2026), building interconnects that link different qubit types (ions, neutral atoms, superconducting) into one networked architecture. His phrasing: the industry moves faster when everyone shares common infrastructure instead of reinventing the wheel.

→ Error correction. The translation layer that turns raw algorithms into instructions a noisy machine can actually execute.

→ Interoperability that already ships. IonQ’s atomic clocks sit inside other labs’ quantum computers. Its photon detectors sit inside photonic stacks. Components, not just systems.

Two lines from him I keep replaying:

“The volume of interest in these quantum computers outpaces the pace and scale at which we can manufacture them.”

“A network that only worked for your Dell PC wouldn’t be nearly as useful as an open one.”

But here’s what actually stuck.

Bhaskar reached for the same analogy De Masi uses. GPUs sat in workstations for years. The AI boom didn’t start because the chip existed. It started the moment GPUs got networked, manufactured at scale, and racked in datacenters. Quantum is sitting on that exact line now.

@NiccoloDeMasi frames the goal as being “the Nvidia AND Cisco of quantum.” Bhaskar, from the R&D side, just described the same company from inside the lab. 

When the CEO’s pitch and the head of R&D’s roadmap are the same sentence, it stops being a soundbite and becomes a strategy.

The race everyone watches is for a better qubit. IonQ is running the one nobody photographs the missing middle. That’s usually the race that decides who’s still standing when the hype clears.

Must Watch  👇📹

#IonQ #QuantumComputing #EconQuantum

The story wasn’t a faster qubit. It was the “missing middle.” Mihir Bhaskar, IonQ’s SVP Global R&D and the Lightsynq founder who co-authored this year’s Nature paper on entanglement-assisted interferometry, sat with @TheEconomist Tom Standage at Commercialising Quantum Global. His reframing: the question has moved from IF quantum works to HOW it scales. And the hard part is no longer any single computer. It’s the infrastructure that doesn’t exist yet: the connective tissue between a lab result and a real industry. He calls it the missing middle. → Manufacturing. IonQ’s bet here is the SkyWater foundry (acquisition announced, still pending FTC clearance), which Bhaskar framed as compressing the chip design-and-verification loop from roughly 6 months to roughly 3 weeks. And SkyWater builds for trapped-ion, superconducting and photonic platforms alike, not only IonQ’s own. → Networking standards. IonQ is in DARPA’s HARQ program (awarded April 2026), building interconnects that link different qubit types (ions, neutral atoms, superconducting) into one networked architecture. His phrasing: the industry moves faster when everyone shares common infrastructure instead of reinventing the wheel. → Error correction. The translation layer that turns raw algorithms into instructions a noisy machine can actually execute. → Interoperability that already ships. IonQ’s atomic clocks sit inside other labs’ quantum computers. Its photon detectors sit inside photonic stacks. Components, not just systems. Two lines from him I keep replaying: “The volume of interest in these quantum computers outpaces the pace and scale at which we can manufacture them.” “A network that only worked for your Dell PC wouldn’t be nearly as useful as an open one.” But here’s what actually stuck. Bhaskar reached for the same analogy De Masi uses. GPUs sat in workstations for years. The AI boom didn’t start because the chip existed. It started the moment GPUs got networked, manufactured at scale, and racked in datacenters. Quantum is sitting on that exact line now. @NiccoloDeMasi frames the goal as being “the Nvidia AND Cisco of quantum.” Bhaskar, from the R&D side, just described the same company from inside the lab. When the CEO’s pitch and the head of R&D’s roadmap are the same sentence, it stops being a soundbite and becomes a strategy. The race everyone watches is for a better qubit. IonQ is running the one nobody photographs the missing middle. That’s usually the race that decides who’s still standing when the hype clears. Everyone owns the obvious AI winners.

The real money is often made one layer deeper.

Photonics, optical networking, co-packaged optics, AI interconnects, testing infrastructure—this is where the next bottlenecks are forming.

Of the 20 companies on this map, I'm only invested in 2:

✅ $AAOI
✅ $AEHR

One is helping solve AI networking demand.
The other could become a critical beneficiary of silicon photonics testing.

I recently completed a deep dive on $AAOI for anyone wanting to go deeper.  https://t.co/kjI5qF2wF3

Would love to hear which names YOU think have the biggest upside from the photonics revolution.

#AI #Stocks #Photonics #AAOI #AEHR #Investing

text:
Serenity 今天主动公开了自己的亏损仓位，这在财经博主里非常罕见，值得认真读一遍。
她的三大核心主线是：新型云计算（能源基础设施）、存储（Memory）、光子学/光模块（Photonics）。
先说哪些赚了：
在这三条主线里，她选中了各自赛道的最强标的——新型云计算选了 NBIS（美股，AI数据中心能源基础设施），存储选了NBIS（美股，AI数据中心能源基础设施），存储选了EWY LEAPS 期权（美股，追踪韩国股市的 ETF，覆盖三星和 SK 海力士），光模块选了 $SIVE（瑞典股市，硅光子核心供应商）。这三个都是今年的顶级表现者。
再说哪些亏了，这部分更有价值：
第一个大坑：台湾 CPO（光电共封装）产业链。 今年初有分析师发布了一份关于"CPO 延迟"的报告，引发市场恐慌，导致她持有的三只台湾 CPO 相关标的（Foci、Msscorp、Xintec）大幅下跌，目前仍处于深度亏损。后来英伟达直接出面否认了 CPO 延迟的说法，但股价已经被砸坏了。 ——这个教训对 A 股投资者很有参考意义：一份分析师报告，哪怕后来被证明是错的，也可以在短期内把一个正确的产业逻辑打成重伤。
第二个大坑：韩国标的。 她持有的 Auros 和 Foosung（WF₆六氟化钨供应商）目前都是亏损状态，韩国市场的波动性让她直呼"PTSD"，表示短期内可能不会再碰韩国股票。但她同时说，长期来看，随着 SK 海力士和三星完成量产资质认证（HVM），以及日本供应链的潜在收缩，这些标的最终可能还是会回到盈利区间。
第三个坑：宏观判断失误。 她之前做多过公用事业 ETF XLU（美股，电力/能源基础设施），逻辑是降息预期。但伊朗战争爆发后，降息预期从3−4次直接归零，这个仓位没有按预期走。此外，XLU（美股，电力/能源基础设施），逻辑是降息预期。但伊朗战争爆发后，降息预期从3−4次直接归零，这个仓位没有按预期走。此外，TTD（美股，程序化广告平台）和 $META（美股，社交媒体/广告）也是亏损收场。
最后她说了一句非常重要的话：
"我提到 AAOI的时候是30美元，AAOI的时候是30美元，AXTI 是 13 美元，但不是每个人的买入价都一样。如果有人在 220 美元买了 $AAOI，然后跌到 160 美元，我会感到很抱歉。"
这句话直接点出了跟单最大的风险：博主发现机会的时间点，和你看到这条推文的时间点，可能已经相差了几十甚至几百个百分点。
对我们 A 股投资者，这条推文有三个实际参考价值：
第一，即便是研究深度极强的博主，也会踩坑。Serenity 的整体胜率很高，但她仍然有台湾 CPO 链、韩国标的、宏观判断等多个方向的亏损。没有人能做到全对，这是市场的本质。
第二，"分析师报告"不等于事实。台湾 CPO 延迟报告的案例说明，一份被广泛传播的研报，可以在短期内制造巨大的价格扭曲，而最终被证伪的时候，损失已经造成。对于 A 股里同样的 CPO/光模块标的，这个逻辑同样适用。
第三，买入时机比方向判断更重要。方向对了，但买在高点，依然可能亏损。Serenity 自己也承认，她更倾向于用几个月的中期维度来评判一个想法，而不是几周。这和 A 股里很多人追热点、几天就割肉的操作方式形成了鲜明对比。

I think something to highlight also is not all my ideas are green, especially on short term timeframes!

My core three themes are Neoclouds (Energy), Memory, and Photonics. 

And I'm glad I chose the literal top performers for each segment from $NBIS to $EWY leaps to $SIVE.

However, I still have pretty large losses following the false analyst report on CPO delays that $NVDA refuted: That nuked 3 of my TW CPO longs from Foci, Msscorp, Xintec and others. (which are heavily red). Shunsin / Win Semi are holding up much better though. Some of the other ones in Japan that I've mentioned like Towa, Harmonic Drive, NCI, etc. are performing much better following short-term volatility. As for Korea yes I have PTSD for from Auros and now Foosung that are both both red (I didn't own 093370 though, just got PTSD watching price action). Idk if I'll touch Korea again, just way too volatile. But I still think those will end up green eventually following Sk hynix/samsung qualifications + HVM, and the future japanese supply chain shutdown. I also did change some of my previous long ideas like $XLU following the Iran War nuking all chances of rate cuts from 3-4 down to 0, and that didn't play out too well. Three software names I mentioned previously like $TTD, $META ended up red. $SNAP, I also changed my thesis after noticing the endless SBC accounting methods. $RDDT idea was finally in the green from $130 -> $170 after a long time. But I think the vast majority of my ideas like $MRVL, $NBIS, $ARM, $INTC, $MU, $LITE, SK Hynix, Samsung, for larger cap. Down to $AXTI, $LITE, $AAOI, $RPI, $IQE, and others for smaller cap positions directionally play out pretty well. With random stuff like $SIMO, $HPS.A, $TSEM, $AEHR, $LPK, $SOI, $ALRIB, and so on all ended up turning out aight too. The blended average is kinda overwhelmingly green since majority of my long ideas are triple digit YTD. But I've definitely missed a few. Also entry point is really important too... I mentioned $AAOI at $30 or $AXTI at ~$13, but not everyone has the same entry point. So if someone bought AOI at $220 and it dropped to $160, I'd feel bad. text:
VIA Technologies is going hard on AI, targeting 3nm ASIC projects with TSMC.

Chairman Chen Wen-chi says the AI wave is like a “tech tsunami” memory, advanced nodes, packaging, and optical components all in tight supply (even worse than pandemic times). 

Also pushing optical comms/silicon photonics for AI data centers, new edge AI solutions (VISVIA brand), and more.

AI demand still in early stages according to them. Positioning for the long haul.

text:
$IONQ President Trump just signed a Quantum Executive Order today. This isn't a press release. This isn't a pilot program. This is the U.S. government officially declaring quantum computing a national strategic priority — and $IONQ is sitting at the center of it.

The EO mandates a national goal to build the first quantum computer powerful enough to unlock "quantum-enabled scientific discovery." It directs the Department of Energy, Department of Defense, Commerce, and the Intelligence Community to coordinate a whole-of-government push. It calls for quantum-enabled sensors and networks deployed within the next five years. It creates National Quantum Workforce Development Institutes. And it reconstitutes the National Quantum Initiative Advisory Committee while expanding the Quantum Counterintelligence Protection Team.

Back in May, the Trump administration dropped $2 billion in quantum grants and equity stakes across nine companies. $IONQ wasn't on that list. The stock still jumped 12% on the news. Why? Because investors weren't buying individual winners — they were buying the thesis that quantum is now a national priority. Today's Executive Order takes that thesis and puts it in writing at the highest level of the U.S. government.

So why does this matter specifically for $IONQ?

Three reasons.

First, trapped-ion is the architecture that matters most when accuracy is the requirement. IonQ's technology starts with fewer errors and higher gate fidelity than superconducting approaches. When you're building a system designed for "quantum-enabled scientific discovery," you can't afford errors at scale. Especially with government mass and military operations that are national security dependent. Trapped-ion gives you the accuracy foundation that other architectures are still trying to engineer their way toward.

Second, domestic supply chain is now a mandate — and IonQ already built one. The acquisition of $SKYT will solidify this. The EO explicitly directs the development of adequate domestic supply chain and manufacturing capabilities for quantum technologies. $IONQ already working with $SKYT Technology, a U.S.-based foundry, to keep its quantum supply chain on American soil. They built this before it was required. That's not a coincidence — that's a company that understood where government priorities were heading and positioned accordingly.

Third, the government equity omission from May may be temporary. Prediction markets on Kalshi (Not that it matters) are currently placing odds that IonQ receives a direct U.S. government equity stake in 2026. For context, that's roughly the same odds given to $RGTI — and Rigetti was included in the May round. The door is not closed.

Now let's talk about where $IONQ actually stands fundamentally, because headlines don't pay the bills — execution does.

IonQ reported $130 million in revenue for full-year 2025, making it the first pure-play quantum computing company to cross the $100 million revenue threshold. Management has guided for approximately $270 million in 2026 — nearly doubling year-over-year at the midpoint. Wall Street consensus projects revenue surpassing $1.4 billion by 2030. The company also reported $470 million in remaining performance obligations as of May 2026, which signals strong forward revenue visibility — this isn't speculative pipeline, it's contracted backlog.

On the technology roadmap, IonQ is currently rolling out its fifth-generation Tempo system and has a sixth-generation 256-qubit platform in development. That next-gen system uses electronic controls instead of lasers — simpler architecture, lower cost, more scalable. The acquisition of Oxford Ionics was designed to accelerate exactly this transition. The long-term roadmap targets 10,000 qubits and, eventually, two million physical qubits by 2030. The EO calls for a computer powerful enough to initiate the era of quantum-enabled scientific discovery. IonQ published a roadmap to get there. Most peers haven't.

Northland raised its price target on $IONQ to $70 from $55 today, maintaining an Outperform rating. The firm cited confidence that the company's investor day on September 8 will demonstrate its leadership in achieving "Broad Quantum Advantage."

$IONQ is currently trading around $58–$59. The bulls and the skeptics are essentially at the same number right now, which tells you the market is pricing in execution risk alongside the upside. That's actually a more honest setup than a stock that's already priced for perfection.

I want to be transparent about the bear case too, because credibility matters more than hype.

$IONQ remains EBITDA negative with heavy losses continuing despite the revenue growth. The May $2 billion round excluded them — some analysts have called that a "comparative disadvantage" for companies not receiving the government's implicit endorsement. And the valuation at roughly 73x forward price-to-sales requires a lot of things to go right. None of this is a reason to ignore what happened today. But it is a reason to size positions based on your own risk tolerance rather than headlines.

Today's EO is also not just an $IONQ story.

The entire quantum sector just received a presidential mandate. $QBTS received government investment in the May round. $RGTI is superconducting-based and also in that round. $QUBT is a smaller-cap photonics play with higher volatility. the CEO of $INFQ was there as well. $IBM is building the Anderon foundry with $1 billion in government backing — America's first purpose-built quantum foundry. The EO calls for a whole-of-government approach across DOE, DoD, Commerce, and the Intelligence Community. That's broad enough to benefit every serious player in the space. The rising tide thesis just got a signed Executive Order behind it.

Prediction markets on Kalshi (Not that it matters) are currently placing odds that IonQ receives a direct U.S. government equity stake in 2026. For context, that's roughly the same odds given to $RGTI — and Rigetti was included in the May round. The door is not closed. Now let's talk about where $IONQ actually stands fundamentally, because headlines don't pay the bills — execution does. IonQ reported $130 million in revenue for full-year 2025, making it the first pure-play quantum computing company to cross the $100 million revenue threshold. Management has guided for approximately $270 million in 2026 — nearly doubling year-over-year at the midpoint. Wall Street consensus projects revenue surpassing $1.4 billion by 2030. The company also reported $470 million in remaining performance obligations as of May 2026, which signals strong forward revenue visibility — this isn't speculative pipeline, it's contracted backlog. On the technology roadmap, IonQ is currently rolling out its fifth-generation Tempo system and has a sixth-generation 256-qubit platform in development. That next-gen system uses electronic controls instead of lasers — simpler architecture, lower cost, more scalable. The acquisition of Oxford Ionics was designed to accelerate exactly this transition. The long-term roadmap targets 10,000 qubits and, eventually, two million physical qubits by 2030. The EO calls for a computer powerful enough to initiate the era of quantum-enabled scientific discovery. IonQ published a roadmap to get there. Most peers haven't. Northland raised its price target on $IONQ to $70 from $55 today, maintaining an Outperform rating. The firm cited confidence that the company's investor day on September 8 will demonstrate its leadership in achieving "Broad Quantum Advantage." $IONQ is currently trading around $58–$59. The bulls and the skeptics are essentially at the same number right now, which tells you the market is pricing in execution risk alongside the upside. That's actually a more honest setup than a stock that's already priced for perfection. I want to be transparent about the bear case too, because credibility matters more than hype. $IONQ remains EBITDA negative with heavy losses continuing despite the revenue growth. The May $2 billion round excluded them — some analysts have called that a "comparative disadvantage" for companies not receiving the government's implicit endorsement. And the valuation at roughly 73x forward price-to-sales requires a lot of things to go right. None of this is a reason to ignore what happened today. But it is a reason to size positions based on your own risk tolerance rather than headlines. Today's EO is also not just an $IONQ story. The entire quantum sector just received a presidential mandate. $QBTS received government investment in the May round. $RGTI is superconducting-based and also in that round. $QUBT is a smaller-cap photonics play with higher volatility. the CEO of $INFQ was there as well. $IBM is building the Anderon foundry with $1 billion in government backing — America's first purpose-built quantum foundry. The EO calls for a whole-of-government approach across DOE, DoD, Commerce, and the Intelligence Community. That's broad enough to benefit every serious player in the space. text:
$SIVE quietly has a consumer sensing business almost nobody is modeling. Their own product page spells it out.

→ DFB lasers for "biometric and health monitoring"

→ Target customers: "OEMs and device manufacturers developing optical sensing modules for wearables, AR/VR systems, health monitors, and smart home devices"

→ Output power up to 100mW across 1200-2400nm wavelengths

→ Ultra-low FIT rates (<1) — the reliability bar consumer electronics demand

→ Optimized for hybrid silicon photonics integration
Why these specs matter:

The 1200-2400nm range is the band used for optical biosensing through skin — the exact wavelengths relevant to non-invasive health monitoring like blood glucose and blood oxygen. The sub-1 FIT rate is the failure tolerance a company shipping tens of millions of consumer units would require.

Now connect it to what the company has said:

→ CEO Vathulya: "two technologies feeding three supercycles"

→ Management has repeatedly flagged sensing and biosensing as optionality the market isn't pricing

Here's the discipline point: Sivers names no customers on this page. None. So anyone telling you exactly who's buying is guessing.

What's NOT a guess: $SIVE has a productized, consumer-grade, health-sensing laser line, built to the reliability spec that mass-market wearables require, sitting inside a company the market is valuing almost entirely on its AI datacenter story.

The wearables/biosensing leg is real. It's just not priced.

$SIVE

text:
🪲 How do #beetles create vivid colours without pigments?

Join Dr. Ahu Gümrah Dumanli-Parry (🇬🇧University of Manchester) for a #seminar hosted by Dr. Emily Cranston on nature-inspired #photonic #materials. Learn how self-assembling cellulose-based systems can recreate optical architectures found in beetles and provide new routes to scalable, bio-inspired functional materials.

📅 Friday, June 26, 12 PM
📍 CHBE 202, UBC Vancouver
🔗Register: https://t.co/5VPZ1WI1Tv

#MaterialsScience #Photonics #Biomaterials #BioInspiredDesign #UBC
@ubcappscience @UBCForestry @ubcLFS @CHBEUBC @ubcscience @ubcengineering  @UBC @ubcchem

text:
Portfolio update:

Today was one of those days where doing nothing was the trade.

No covered calls.
No CSPs.
No chasing.
No trimming.
Just let the AI infra book work.
Daily P&L: +2.74%
Open P&L: +87.26%
Cash: 21.17%
Current allocation:
$NBIS — 27.11%
Largest position, red today but still up big overall. Core neocloud position.
$MU — 21.71%
Monster day into earnings week. Anthropic partnership added even more confirmation to the memory thesis.

Cash — 21.17%
Still keeping dry powder. Cash is a weapon, not a drag.
$MRVL — 8.07%
Laggard today, but still a core AI interconnect/custom silicon position.
$CRDO — 6.79%
Huge day. Connectivity trade is alive and well.

$DRAM — 6.27%
Memory basket continues to work with $MU.

$VRT — 4.28%
Power/cooling still confirming the AI data center buildout.
$COHR — 3.36%
Photonics/laser chokepoint had a monster move.

$ALAB — 1.31%
Still too small. I want more over time, but not chasing vertical candles.
Big lesson today:

The clean book won.
No options capping the winners.
No obligations forcing decisions.
No overtrading.

Sometimes the most professional move is sitting on your hands while the thesis plays out.

text:
Many people ask why I am aggressively positioned across this specific supply chain: $SIVE, $AAOI, ShunSin, and FOCI.

​The biggest bottleneck in AI is no longer software, it’s physics. For the next generation of compute to function, datacenters must solve a critical equation:

1-Consume less power.

2-Generate less heat.

3- Multiply data bandwidth.

​Copper cables have hit their physical limits (too slow, too hot, too power-hungry). The only absolute structural solution is CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) and Silicon Photonics: replacing electricity with light.

​Here is why this exact basket of stocks is the literal chokepoint of this revolution:

​🔹 $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) - The Engine:

To have optics, you need a light source. Sivers manufactures the DFB laser arrays. Their specific architecture is the only one capable of providing the necessary power to hyperscalers while massively reducing the thermal footprint. It's the ignition.

​🔹 FOCI - The Optical Bridge:

Generating light is useless if you can't route it with surgical precision. FOCI is the leader in fiber arrays and micro-optic coupling. They connect this light to the silicon with zero signal loss.

​🔹 ShunSin - The Architect (Advanced Packaging):

Integrating lasers and fibers directly onto a chip requires atomic-level assembly technology (OSAT). ShunSin holds the advanced packaging expertise to assemble all these optical components as close to the AI processor as physically possible.

​🔹 $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) - The Deployment:

Once the chips are equipped, millions of servers need to be connected. AAOI mass-produces the ultra-high-speed optical transceivers and network infrastructure so the entire datacenter can communicate at the speed of light.

​While the retail herd chases chip designers at absurd valuations, smart money is quietly cornering the hardware bottleneck that is physically required for AI to scale by 2027.

​I don't trade hope. I trade physics. 🐺💡

#SiliconPhotonics #AI #CPO #Tech

Position early and let the decade do the work. AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it. Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier. Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T. Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward. Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI. Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins. Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group. Seven sectors. One direction. text:
CPO 연재 총정리 — 빛이 칩 안으로 들어오는 순간

6편에 걸친 CPO(Co-Packaged Optics) 연재의 요약본. 기술 핵심은 짧게, 투자 함의는 무게감 있게

핵심 메시지

1. CPO는 "되느냐 안 되느냐"의 기술이 아니라 "언제, 어디서부터 되느냐"의 기술이다.

2. AI 추론은 연산보다 데이터 이동에 전력을 더 많이 쓴다. ASIC 전력의 최대 절반이 칩 간 데이터 이동에 들어간다.

3. 구리는 전력벽(power wall)에 도달했다. 짧은 거리에서는 광학이 답이지만, 광학 부품도 전자 회로만큼 작아져야 그 이득을 실제로 누릴 수 있다.

4. Nvidia 로드맵은 2단계다. 2026년 Rubin(Spectrum6 스위치)에서 스케일아웃에 먼저 CPO 적용, 2028년 Feynman(NVLink8)에서 스케일업까지 확장.

5.같은 데이터를 보고도 Nvidia·Meta는 "이제 시작"이라 하고, Google·Broadcom은 "아직 아니다"라고 한다. 벤더 락인 우려와 2018년 온보드 옵틱스(OBO) 실패 경험이 후자의 신중론을 뒷받침한다.

기술 검증: 3가지 Kill Condition

6. 파이버 정렬 — 광파이버(8~10μm)와 실리콘 도파관(500nm급)을 50nm 오차 안에서 붙여야 한다. 일반 전기 정렬보다 100배 정밀해야 한다. 해법은 여러 개 동시에 검증되는 단계까지 왔지만, 표준이 하나로 수렴하지 않았다.

7. 레이저 신뢰성 — 생산된 레이저의 최대 50%가 스펙 미달이다. 업계는 ELSFP(레이저를 패키지 밖으로 분리)로 리스크를 줄이고, 중복화(Lightmatter)·다중화(Ranovus 퀀텀닷) 두 방향으로 신뢰성을 끌어올리는 중이다.

8. 테스트 데이터 통합 — 전기(STDF)와 광학(CSV/XLS) 검사 데이터가 서로 다른 포맷으로 따로 쌓인다. 전기 99%×광학 99% = 98% 수율로, 곱셈 구조가 양산 규모에서 손실을 키운다. 업계 중론은 표준화에 최소 2~3년 추가 소요.

9. 세 조건 중 가장 화려하지 않은 3번(테스트 데이터)이 CPO 전체 일정을 가장 오래 잡아끌 가능성이 높다.

투자 관점 — 누가 돈을 버는가

10. 파운드리 — $TSM: 패키지 안 모든 칩렛이 TSMC PDK·공정을 따라야 하는 구조적 락인. Nvidia·AMD·Google·AWS 가속기가 TSMC 패키징을 쓰는 한 광학 엔진 칩렛도 자동으로 TSMC산이 될 가능성. 다만 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 파운드리 시장은 연 10억 달러 미만으로 TSMC 매출의 1%도 안 됨 — 8배 성장 전망이 있지만 출발선 자체가 작다는 점은 균형 있게 봐야 함.

11. 레이저 — $COHR, $LITE: Coherent·Lumentum·Sumitomo 3사가 글로벌 레이저 시장 68% 점유. 레이저 시장은 2024년 200억 달러 돌파, 2030년 300억 달러 이상 전망. $NVDA가 두 회사에 각각 20억 달러 투자해 캐파 선점 — 공급이 수요를 못 따라가는 구간에서 자본이 먼저 줄을 선 신호. Lumentum 자체 추정 InP 광 레인 수요 CAGR 약 85%, 캐파 증설 속도가 계속 못 따라잡는 격차 존재.

12. OCS(광 회로 스위치) — 신흥 전장: CignalAI 추정 TAM 2028~2029년 연 30억 달러 이상. Google 외주화로 Lumentum·Coherent의 매출 기회가 몇 분기 만에 1억→4억 달러로 점프, 단일 고객 10억 달러 계약도 등장. Lumentum R300(MEMS 기반)이 검증된 강자, iPronics 같은 웨이브가이드(SiP) 신생 기업이 정면 도전 — CPO 본류보다 승자가 더 빨리 가려질 수 있는 영역.

13. 시장 규모 종합: pluggable 광모듈 시장 2023년 60억→2030년 250억 달러(Coherent), 광 컴포넌트 시장 2030년 250억 달러(OMDIA)/2029년 310억 달러(CignalAI), 패키징 포함 실리콘 포토닉스 IC 시장 2031~2034년 290억~320억 달러(마진율 50%+ 전망).

14. 인프라 자본 집약도: Ayar Labs·Wiwynn 시연 랙 기준 100kW 이상 전력 밀도, 100% 액체 냉각, HVDC 전력 아키텍처 필요. CPO 도입은 칩 설계 문제를 넘어 랙 전체 전력·냉각 인프라 교체를 요구하는 자본 집약적 사업.

15. 지정학 변수: 중국은 150mm 리튬나이오베이트 웨이퍼 자체 개발(CHIPX), 일본 257억 달러 투입, 인도 포토닉스 팹 최대 50% 자본 지원, 유럽은 M&A로 규모 확대 중. 인력 부족(2025년 상반기 R&D 공석 330개+)과 CEO 교체(IPG Photonics, Lumentum)는 업계 리더십 불안정성의 신호.

$TSM $COHR $LITE $NVDA 

매수, 매도 등 투자권유 아님.

text:
It’s one thing to argue AI equities are in a bubble, but another to suggest the underlying buildout isn’t real.

The valuation argument may be correct, but it’s separate from the physical reality underneath.

Power, fabrication, photonics, and heat rejection remain scarce, capital-intensive, and difficult to scale.

Stocks can fall without changing what’s physically been built.

text:
totally agree on "IMO Photonics is the next supercycle but we’re closer to the beginning, a lot of the major revenue ramps begin mid 2027."

The market has already started to price in the theme, yet many of the real revenue ramps are not expected to begin until mid-2027 and beyond. That means the story is less about chasing what has already happened, and more about identifying which parts of the photonics stack will convert today’s technical bottlenecks into revenue over the next several years.

For me, the first places to look have always been the prerequisites for that ramp. Before photonics can become a true revenue supercycle, the industry needs enough manufacturing capacity and enough test infrastructure to support it. That is why I have focused heavily on two layers of the stack: fabs such as $TSEM, $GFS, $TSM, $XFAB, and $UMC, and measurement/testing names such as $FORM, $TER, $VIAV, $ATEYY, $KEYS, and $AEHR.

Of course, the broader opportunity also includes assembly, packaging, substrates, lasers, and many other layers. But in my view, fabs and testing are the two early gates. Without them, the ramp cannot scale; with them, today’s technical demand can begin turning into real, visible revenue.

$POET now officially connected to a top 3 hyperscaler via Lumilens. 

Be patient and know what you own. $POET is by far my current biggest bet in photonics /  AI infrastructure theme and I have been in photonics for over 2 years now. 

I wouldnt be shocked if we surpass $AAOI in marketcap within the next 18 months.

$SIVE AGM & Q1 2026 – The board upgrade that signals M&A readiness right now
 I’ve been reflecting on the AGM outcome and the Q1 transcript, and one thread stands out more clearly than before: this company is deliberately positioning itself for the next layer of acceleration.
 Vickram was direct about it:

“This brings a very experienced and balanced board composition for us, and in particular, tremendous executive credibility and experience with U.S. markets, as well as strong M&A experience as we look not only at organically growing our business, but also adjacencies, complementary portfolios as well for the future if that can help further accelerate growth for the company.”

That’s not just some corporate line. That’s the CEO openly saying they’re keeping the door open for smart acquisitions.

The new board members feel spot on for this phase. Joakim Nideborn as Vice Chairman with his listed-company CFO experience and Helena Svancar with her deep M&A background (including time as Head of M&A at $ERIC) these are exactly the kind of people you want around the table when you’re thinking about scaling faster than organic growth alone can take you.

I’ve seen this story play out with $SIVEF before. Back in 2017 they acquired CST Global, what became Sivers Photonics. It was a bold bet on III-V lasers and the Glasgow foundry. There was dilution, integration headaches, and it took several years before the payoff became obvious. But look where we are today: that same platform is right in the middle of the AI data center laser ramps, LiDAR production ($AEVA), and the $JBL pluggable collaboration. It completely transformed what $SIVE could do.

MixComm followed a similar path, added real wireless depth and U.S. muscle, and now it’s helping drive the SATCOM momentum with https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp / $YSS and the big IRIS² opportunity in Europe.
Both deals took time and patience, but they delivered real acceleration. I have a strong feeling we’re heading for something similar again. The setup just feels right: a nearly $800M pipeline that’s still growing fast, multiple 2027 ramps stacking up, and now a board with genuine M&A firepower. I’m actively digging into potential candidates right now, and when I’ve vetted something properly I’ll share my thoughts.
 What makes this moment particularly interesting is how the transcript frames the bigger picture. Vickram described being in the middle of three super cycles at once AI factories, space/SATCOM, and modern warfare and noted that $SIVE has differentiated technology that can meaningfully support elements of all three. He also highlighted the IRIS² European satellite infrastructure effort as a substantial long-term opportunity for the wireless business: a pipeline north of $80 million through 2030 (and that’s not even half the lifetime revenue potential), with $SIVE already engaged with five subcontractors in the final RFP stage for user terminals that start development in 2027. As an EU-based provider, they’re uniquely positioned on the sovereignty angle.
 The board changes, the explicit openness to adjacencies and M&A, the progress on U.S. dual-listing readiness, and the manufacturing capacity build-out all point in the same direction: $SIVE is building the foundation and the optionality to grow both organically and through smart additions at exactly the moment the secular tailwinds are strongest.
 I remain deeply convicted as a long-term shareholder. The transformation from development-led to product-led is progressing, the pipeline is a genuine lead indicator, and the company now has the governance and expertise in place to act on opportunities that can meaningfully accelerate the trajectory.
 Small volumes turning into bigger numbers and potentially even bigger ones, faster, with the right moves.

What are your thoughts? Did anything in the transcript or the board changes stand out to you? Or any areas you think would make sense for a potential acquisition? Would love to hear!

text:
My thesis was that stocks top with $SPCX.

Why?

Stock bros up 20x, crypto bros buying stock bros bags at ATH’s; the very same “retail” that was crushed at the top of every crypto bull cycle…

Everyone following stock KOL’s like @aleabitoreddit and ape whatever ticker is mentioned.

Trillion dollar IPO’s.

Literally the same sh** we’ve seen at the top of every crypto bull cycle.

Of course, the difference is these are real companies with real revenue, but timing does still matter unless you want to sit -30% under water for a couple years and HODL.

And at the end of the day, businesses fail, competition is fierce, there’s no way every single one of your bottleneck photonics glass substrate hydro-cooling picks are going to just keep going up over time.

Everyone’s a genius season- notice it when it’s there and start looking for the exit.

text:
If you're not buying today there's probably no reason to be checking your portfolio every five minutes
Close the app and go outside

It's always refreshing being around people in the real world who have absolutely no idea the market is down

I don't think this is the start of a bear market or some epic crash. If anything, the market seems to be getting a little ahead of itself on rate cut expectations

$MU reports tonight and I think it'll be one of the more important reads for the AI and photonics trade

Volatility is the price you pay for superior returns
It sucks but that's the game we play

text:
Personally looking at Quantum Technology "RIGID" stocks

here's what I think overall

May 21st, $2 Billion+ was committed to the CHIPS and Science Act, plus two executive orders on June 22nd to reinforce this:
> quantum computing got DoE 
> migrate to post-quantum computing by 2030 by agencies

There are 9 companies in US stocks that this can affect directly

The RIGID stocks imo have the higher importance
Rigetti $RGTI 
IMB $IBM 
Global Foundations $GFS 
Infleqion $INFQ 
D-Wave $QBTS 

With these, the two that I have personal interest are IMB and GFS. 

IMB needs to introduction here and technically the money goes to Anderson, but its still the front runner of superconducting for 2028 and having domestic but indirect infrastructure is still a benefit to the company. 

GFS is focused on all quantum based architecture, but committed to AI Data Centers domestically, called SCALE for high bandwidth but energy efficient connectivity 

I have been looking for a company that will release the pressure valve on demand and GFS could do it in the same way that broadband helped de-pressurize the telecom days.

I am not as bullish on QBTS for example, I think they have too much rising debts and revenue fell 81%, but could get a partnership or acquisition. 

RGTI def worth a watch with their $NVDA NVQLink partnership and Cepheus launch 

The other 4 stocks worth mentioning:
Atom Computing 
DIraq
PsiQuantum 
Quantinnum 

These are mainly supply chain stocks for sensors and photonics but could be part of the discussion. They could certainly do well or bad, but I think the RIGIDs would help them rise, so better imo to focus on the main stack. 

Ive heard about quantum technology for a long time

Is it finally here?
